name: Go Microservices
description: Go services with chi router, pgx, slog structured logging, and Docker multi-stage builds
conventions: Package names are short, lowercase, singular — no underscores or camelCase (user, order, auth)
Wrap errors with fmt.Errorf("context: %w", err) — never discard errors or use panic for flow control
Pass context.Context as the first argument to every function that does I/O
Define interfaces at the consumer, not the provider — keep interfaces small (1-3 methods)
No ORM — use pgx directly with typed query functions; raw SQL in const blocks at top of file
Table-driven tests with t.Run() — cover happy path, error cases, and edge cases in one test function
Use slog for structured logging — log.Printf is not allowed in service code
Graceful shutdown via os.Signal channel — always drain in-flight requests before exiting
Makefile for common tasks: make build, make test, make lint, make migrate, make docker-build
Docker multi-stage builds: build stage with golang:alpine, final stage with scratch or distroless
Environment config via os.Getenv with documented required vars — fail fast at startup if missing
HTTP handlers return early on error — avoid deeply nested if-else in handlers
Use sync.WaitGroup and errgroup for concurrent operations — never raw goroutines without tracking
gRPC services defined in .proto files in api/proto/ — generated code in api/gen/ (never edited manually)
